article_meta.title: After some 150 students walked out of Redlands schools early this month in support of immigrants, they were dealt an une

reasoning: The model identifies several genuinely defensible bias instances in the author's own prose. No quoted text is flagged as author bias - all flagged items come from the author's connecting prose and structural choices. The bias type labels are mostly reasonable, though some could be debated. The analysis is thoughtful and identifies real patterns of author-level framing.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All four flagged items are from the author's own prose - no quoted text or attributed statements are flagged as author bias. The model correctly avoids flagging statements from Stephens, Hardy, Aya F, Bojado, Belcher, ACLU guidance, or other quoted sources.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1 (Slant) and 3 (Word Choice) are strong, defensible findings. Item 2 (Bias by Omission) is somewhat of a stretch - the violence is mentioned, and characterizing brief coverage as 'omission' is debatable. Item 4 is a reasonable observation about minimization of the MAGA hat incident, though 'a few tense minutes' is arguably just descriptive. Overall most items are defensible but 1-2 are somewhat questionable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model could have flagged additional instances: the phrase 'immigration enforcement crackdown' is author prose with loaded word choice ('crackdown'); 'the young activists' as a section header has sympathetic framing; 'what they regard as anti-LGBTQ+ policies' uses 'anti-LGBTQ+' which could be seen as the author adopting activist framing. Also 'Student leaders see their protests as a civics lesson in action' is sympathetic author framing. Some of these are minor but a couple are worth flagging.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Slant for the contrast framing is appropriate. Bias by Omission for item 2 is somewhat stretched but defensible. Word Choice for 'ideologically similar' is appropriate. Bias by Story Choice and Placement for item 4 is reasonable - the minimization and placement argument fits this category. Labels are mostly correct or defensibly close.
